consistency.level: Consistent. Level 0 (Cantrip) matches common D&D spell conventions.
consistency.school: Consistent. Evocation is the standard school for offensive damage spells like this.
consistency.casting_time_range_duration: Consistent. '1 action', '120 feet', and 'Instantaneous' are standard values for a basic damage cantrip.
consistency.components: Consistent. V, S (Verbal, Somatic) is typical for a cantrip that doesn't require material components.
consistency.scaling: Mechanically sound. The progression (2 beams at 5th, 3 at 11th, 4 at 17th) is the established scaling for the Eldritch Blast cantrip in 5th Edition D&D, although the damage die (1d10) is noted separately.

quality.completeness: High. Contains all core spell data fields (level, time, range, components, description).
quality.clarity: Very High. The description clearly outlines the attack type (ranged spell attack), damage type (force), and the specific scaling mechanism for higher levels.
quality.formatting: Good. The description uses line breaks effectively to separate the base effect from the scaling effect.

mechanics.scaling_mechanism: The number of attacks (beams) increases at specific levels (5, 11, 17). Each beam requires a separate attack roll.
mechanics.class_omission_note: The 'classes' field is empty. While Eldritch Blast is primarily associated with Warlock, this field should ideally list 'Warlock' (and potentially Sorcerer/Fiend Patron/Magic Initiate sources if this data set tracks spell origins comprehensively).
